req.title: Hr-Admin updates payroll-run
req.statement: An authenticated hr-admin shall be able to update a payroll-run through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: The requirement mandates that an authenticated hr-admin can update a payroll-run via UI, mandatory fields are validated, and an audit record is written on success. I derived: (1) a positive EP/STC test covering the happy-path state transition from existing→updated with audit verification; (2) a NEG/DT test covering missing mandatory fields returning HTTP 400; (3) a BVA test exercising the field-length boundary (max-length name); (4) a NEG test for unauthenticated access returning 401; (5) a NEG/DT test for a forbidden role returning 403. Together these achieve EP, BVA, STC, DT, and NEG coverage as required by ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-4.

tests.name: test_hr_admin_updates_payroll_run_successfully
preconditions: A payroll-run record with id=<PAYROLL_RUN_ID> exists in state DRAFT
actions: Fill all mandatory fields with valid data
Assert HTTP response 200 or 201 with entity id in body
Verify audit record exists for the update action
expected: Payroll-run transitions from DRAFT to UPDATED state
Audit record written with actor=hr-admin, action=UPDATE, entity=payroll-run
trace: R-WEB-038: response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
def test_hr_admin_updates_payroll_run_successfully(hr_admin_page, existing_payroll_run, audit_log_client):
    run_id = existing_payroll_run["id"]
    page = hr_admin_page

    page.goto(f"/payroll-runs/{run_id}/edit")
    <fill_mandatory_fields>(page, {
        "name": "Q2-2024-Payroll",
        "period_start": "2024-04-01",
        "period_end": "2024-06-30",
        "cost_center": "CC-001"
    })
    with page.expect_response(lambda r: f"/payroll-runs/{run_id}" in r.url) as resp_info:
        page.click("[data-testid='submit-btn']")

    response = resp_info.value
    assert response.status in (200, 201), f"Expected 200/201, got {response.status}"

    body = response.json()
    assert "id" in body, "Response body must contain entity id"
    assert str(body["id"]) == str(run_id)

    audit_entries = audit_log_client.get_entries(entity_id=run_id, action="UPDATE")
    assert len(audit_entries) >= 1, "Audit record must be written on successful update"
    latest = audit_entries[-1]
    assert latest["actor_role"] == "hr-admin"
    assert latest["action"] == "UPDATE"

tests.name: test_update_payroll_run_missing_mandatory_fields_returns_400
preconditions: A payroll-run record with id=<PAYROLL_RUN_ID> exists
actions: Clear all mandatory fields (name, period_start, period_end, cost_center)
Assert validation error messages are displayed
expected: Form submission is rejected with HTTP 400
Validation error messages shown for each mandatory field
trace: R-WEB-038: mandatory fields must be validated
tests.executable:
def test_update_payroll_run_missing_mandatory_fields_returns_400(hr_admin_page, existing_payroll_run, audit_log_client):
    run_id = existing_payroll_run["id"]
    page = hr_admin_page

    page.goto(f"/payroll-runs/{run_id}/edit")
    <clear_mandatory_fields>(page, ["name", "period_start", "period_end", "cost_center"])

    audit_count_before = len(audit_log_client.get_entries(entity_id=run_id, action="UPDATE"))

    with page.expect_response(lambda r: f"/payroll-runs/{run_id}" in r.url) as resp_info:
        page.click("[data-testid='submit-btn']")

    response = resp_info.value
    assert response.status == 400, f"Expected 400, got {response.status}"

    for field in ["name", "period_start", "period_end", "cost_center"]:
        error_locator = page.locator(f"[data-testid='error-{field}']")
        assert error_locator.is_visible(), f"Validation error for '{field}' must be visible"

    audit_count_after = len(audit_log_client.get_entries(entity_id=run_id, action="UPDATE"))
    assert audit_count_after == audit_count_before, "No audit record must be written on validation failure"

tests.name: test_update_payroll_run_name_at_max_length_boundary
preconditions: A payroll-run record with id=<PAYROLL_RUN_ID> exists
Max allowed length for 'name' field is 100 characters
actions: Set 'name' field to exactly 100 characters (max boundary)
Fill remaining mandatory fields with valid data
expected: Update succeeds with HTTP 200 or 201 when name is exactly 100 chars
trace: R-WEB-038: mandatory fields must be validated
R-WEB-038: response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
MAX_NAME_LENGTH = 100

def test_update_payroll_run_name_at_max_length_boundary(hr_admin_page, existing_payroll_run, audit_log_client):
    run_id = existing_payroll_run["id"]
    page = hr_admin_page
    boundary_name = "P" * MAX_NAME_LENGTH
    assert len(boundary_name) == MAX_NAME_LENGTH

    page.goto(f"/payroll-runs/{run_id}/edit")
    <fill_mandatory_fields>(page, {
        "name": boundary_name,
        "period_start": "2024-01-01",
        "period_end": "2024-03-31",
        "cost_center": "CC-002"
    })

    with page.expect_response(lambda r: f"/payroll-runs/{run_id}" in r.url) as resp_info:
        page.click("[data-testid='submit-btn']")

    response = resp_info.value
    assert response.status in (200, 201), f"Expected 200/201 at max boundary, got {response.status}"

    body = response.json()
    assert str(body.get("id")) == str(run_id)

    audit_entries = audit_log_client.get_entries(entity_id=run_id, action="UPDATE")
    assert len(audit_entries) >= 1, "Audit record must be written"

    error_locator = page.locator("[data-testid='error-name']")
    assert not error_locator.is_visible(), "No validation error expected at max boundary"

tests.name: test_update_payroll_run_unauthenticated_returns_401
preconditions: User is NOT authenticated (no valid session or token)
A payroll-run record with id=<PAYROLL_RUN_ID> exists
actions: Send PUT/PATCH request to /payroll-runs/<PAYROLL_RUN_ID> without authentication headers
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_update_payroll_run_unauthenticated_returns_401(existing_payroll_run, base_url, audit_log_client):
    run_id = existing_payroll_run["id"]
    audit_count_before = len(audit_log_client.get_entries(entity_id=run_id, action="UPDATE"))

    payload = {
        "name": "Unauthorized-Update",
        "period_start": "2024-01-01",
        "period_end": "2024-03-31",
        "cost_center": "CC-003"
    }
    response = requests.patch(
        f"{base_url}/payroll-runs/{run_id}",
        json=payload,
        headers={"Content-Type": "application/json"}
    )

    assert response.status_code == 401, f"Expected 401, got {response.status_code}"

    audit_count_after = len(audit_log_client.get_entries(entity_id=run_id, action="UPDATE"))
    assert audit_count_after == audit_count_before, "No audit record must be written on 401"

tests.name: test_update_payroll_run_forbidden_role_returns_403
preconditions: User is authenticated with role=employee (not hr-admin)
A payroll-run record with id=<PAYROLL_RUN_ID> exists
actions: Authenticate as a user with role=employee
Send PUT/PATCH request to /payroll-runs/<PAYROLL_RUN_ID> with employee credentials
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_update_payroll_run_forbidden_role_returns_403(existing_payroll_run, base_url, employee_auth_token, audit_log_client):
    run_id = existing_payroll_run["id"]
    audit_count_before = len(audit_log_client.get_entries(entity_id=run_id, action="UPDATE"))

    payload = {
        "name": "Forbidden-Update",
        "period_start": "2024-01-01",
        "period_end": "2024-03-31",
        "cost_center": "CC-004"
    }
    headers = {
        "Content-Type": "application/json",
        "Authorization": f"Bearer {employee_auth_token}"
    }
    response = requests.patch(
        f"{base_url}/payroll-runs/{run_id}",
        json=payload,
        headers=headers
    )

    assert response.status_code == 403, f"Expected 403, got {response.status_code}"

    audit_count_after = len(audit_log_client.get_entries(entity_id=run_id, action="UPDATE"))
    assert audit_count_after == audit_count_before, "No audit record must be written on 403"

tests.name: test_update_payroll_run_name_exceeds_max_length_returns_400
preconditions: A payroll-run record with id=<PAYROLL_RUN_ID> exists
Max allowed length for 'name' field is 100 characters
actions: Fill remaining mandatory fields with valid data
Assert HTTP 400 and a validation error for 'name' are returned
expected: HTTP 400 is returned when name exceeds max length by 1
trace: R-WEB-038: mandatory fields must be validated
tests.executable:
MAX_NAME_LENGTH = 100

def test_update_payroll_run_name_exceeds_max_length_returns_400(hr_admin_page, existing_payroll_run, audit_log_client):
    run_id = existing_payroll_run["id"]
    page = hr_admin_page
    over_boundary_name = "P" * (MAX_NAME_LENGTH + 1)
    assert len(over_boundary_name) == MAX_NAME_LENGTH + 1

    page.goto(f"/payroll-runs/{run_id}/edit")
    audit_count_before = len(audit_log_client.get_entries(entity_id=run_id, action="UPDATE"))

    <fill_mandatory_fields>(page, {
        "name": over_boundary_name,
        "period_start": "2024-01-01",
        "period_end": "2024-03-31",
        "cost_center": "CC-005"
    })

    with page.expect_response(lambda r: f"/payroll-runs/{run_id}" in r.url) as resp_info:
        page.click("[data-testid='submit-btn']")

    response = resp_info.value
    assert response.status == 400, f"Expected 400 for name > max length, got {response.status}"

    error_locator = page.locator("[data-testid='error-name']")
    assert error_locator.is_visible(), "Validation error for 'name' must be visible"

    audit_count_after = len(audit_log_client.get_entries(entity_id=run_id, action="UPDATE"))
    assert audit_count_after == audit_count_before, "No audit record must be written on 400"
